review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: I have been working in Flipspaces for over 7 months. I started off as an intern and with the guidance and support from my peers and managers, I've been able to hone my skills and get promoted to Operation fulfillment executive within a span of few months. What's great about the firm is the culture! Not just within my core team and department but in general overall. The folks around have the same goals and vision as myself - of doing something new and learning on a daily basis and yet super friendly! Over here at Flipspaces, what sets them apart is the way everyone is hustling together as a family rather than a team and making it fun. You get to explore new initiatives which otherwise you wouldn't abe able to do in any other firm i guess. It's not just a 9-5 job you feel you're at, its more about of learning a new skill set, developing yourself as an individual in an enviroment which makes you feel included and makes you feel at home! I have been taking responsibilities to train and hire new talents as interns in my department as well by conducting interviews and screenings. This has helped me uplift my confidence and communication skills.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Technological Pioneering: RIL (Reliance Industries Limited) has consistently demonstrated its commitment to technological innovation. The company has made significant strides in various sectors, including telecommunications, petrochemicals, retail, and energy. RIL's investments in cutting-edge technologies have helped revolutionize industries and establish itself as a leader in multiple domains. Strong Ethical Business Practices: RIL upholds high standards of ethical business practices, setting an example for other organizations. The company maintains transparency in its operations, complies with regulatory requirements, and places a strong emphasis on corporate social responsibility. RIL's dedication to sustainable development, environmental conservation, and community welfare showcases its commitment to making a positive impact beyond its business objectives. Diverse Portfolio and Market Dominance: RIL's extensive portfolio spans multiple sectors, allowing it to diversify its revenue streams and mitigate risks. This diversified approach, combined with the company's strategic vision, has positioned RIL as a dominant force in the market. RIL's ability to identify and capitalize on emerging opportunities has propelled its growth and contributed to its strong financial performance. Employee Development and Well-being: RIL places significant importance on employee development and well-being. The company offers comprehensive training programs, encourages skill enhancement, and provides ample growth opportunities for its workforce. RIL's supportive work environment, competitive compensation packages, and employee welfare initiatives contribute to high employee satisfaction and retention.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Lack of Transparency in Some Areas: While RIL generally maintains transparency in its operations, there have been instances where the company's decision-making processes or project updates have not been adequately communicated to the public or stakeholders. Improved transparency in all aspects would further enhance RIL's reputation as a trustworthy and reliable organization. Environmental Concerns: As a large conglomerate with operations in diverse sectors, RIL faces scrutiny regarding its environmental impact. While the company has made efforts to invest in sustainable practices and initiatives, some stakeholders argue that RIL could do more to mitigate its environmental footprint and actively pursue renewable energy sources.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: If you show the Intent to work there's a decent if not great chances for your growth. The best thing about their petrochemical business is the technology you get to work on, It is almost the best or among the best you can find anywhere & the company's attitude towards safety is also good.  There is way too much to learn right from the technicians to the those who have worked there for over 10+ years. There is no end to learning that's all I can say. Also If you get a chance to work in shutdown or comissioning that knowledge could land you great jobs anywhere in the middle east or India (depending upon the market & oil prices obviously) . All in all you learn a lot.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: There is a considerable difference in almost all aspects of work between the sites the were established by RIL & the ones they acquired from IPCL & also more importantly Jamnagar & other sites. I was unlike to not get an opportunity to work at the Jamnagar site. Often there are chances that due to manpower shortage you may be asked to work another shift like a 16 hr or 12 hr shift instead of 8 hr shift. This is not an issue but when this gets coupled with a life at a remote location with the nothing outside township It can sometimes be very frustrating . Also If you are the kind of a person who believes in following strict sleeping or eating pattern at fixed or proper times in the day. Sorry this is not the place for you.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Reliance is an engineering Marvel. Period. There is no structure in this world as complicated, and simultaneously as easily accessible as Reliance refinery.

While you are there, you may find it really pathetic and will constantly question yourself -- What am I learning here?
But its only when you leave Reliance and join in elsewhere, you find that by merely visiting Refinery field daily for year or two, you have learnt so so much more than rest of your peers.
Reliance Jamnagar refinery is a Big ocean of knowledge...everytime you go into the field, you will find something new.
If you foresee a future as an engineer, this is the right place to start with.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Come to Reliance Refinery only for the sake of learning; dont expect much of salary or any other major perks (except for a good residency complex). HR is too uneducated to make any logical decisions and is over-governed solely by the principal of 'cost-cutting' without much scope for reasoning. Rest, other employees are all good.

Also, do not come to Reliance if you want to pursue MBA from premier institutes; Reliance experience wont be helpful at all.

Thirdly, it is a very large organisation...around 5000 employee in Jamnagar itself...getting promoted to higher levels is very very difficult. May take you 30 years and still you wont climb to top of refinery management leadership team.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: There are good HR benefits.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
There is no freedom to work. I mean you cannot able to install softwares on your machine, you have to get permissions from IT which located in Mumbai. Even for installing a any small supporting application you have to reach local support team then you have to write mail to Mumbai IT team. Then you have to wait for the response saying you are not allowed to install (most of the time). You will 
not be able to download Maven dependencies, for that you have to use your own network. There wont be proper fast network available most of the time. You have to work with eclipse, Intellij software is not  available. You cannot use your personal Email. You have to work with Windows machine (you may get Mac if you are lucky).  You cannot have non veg from office. We are not using  open source . Work progress is very very slow. If your expectation is to work on latest technologies and  improve your skills and knowledge, please never choose. If you just want to work on 9 to 5 basis and not looking for a place after this then you can.   From  my last 2 calibrations, its just a simple process not based on work it is simply manager's  decision.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: pretty good facilities Gym , stadium, medical, monthly activities, continues training programs to develop.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Cons are many 
Make sure you ask for in hand salary before joining, lot of deductions in salary. 
If your package is 8.5. That means you will get in hand 42 k monthly( kind of example). They will hold your 10 percentage of your complete CTC, which  you will get after completing one  year,in this also they will deduct money or some time you will not even going to get anything because they called it performance link incentive. They have grading system when they give this money to you. It s totally up to your boss, what grade he or she given to you so you can get your agreed salary.🙄
Growth is slow.
Without giving notice period they will transfer you from one department to another. To make sure you won't get chance of interview outside of organization. Again you have to spend minimum 1 year in different department to apply somewhere else.

They will dump you with work but not going to increase salary.

They say they have flexi timing but boss will make sure you will come @ 8:30 am ☺️

Worst part is Reliance gives a feeling of government office, where people take 3 days to complete single work, which can be completed in 1 hr.

Most of the people I see in this company they hardly have any work in whole day, best work they do playing games , chatting, watching movies etc . Very few people who are actually contributing for company.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Work life balance in reliance hydrocarbon section is good. Colleagues are quite supportive and ample time is given to complete a task. 
There are frequent recreational activities also held which helps in team building and freshening up one's mind. 
On the work front, most of the times there are no new projects that come up. Major portion of the work includes Modification of their existing plants at different sites which doesn't let one to explore other innovative things , but since work pressure is not much, one can invest time in self development and learning as sufficient softwares are provided.
Canteens are good but then a bit on the costlier side.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
The organisation needs to change some HR policies. 
For instance, the medical insurance offered is mandatory for every employee. The amount deducted from the salary is too high for this. Plus they have made it mandatory for employees to take the top-up plan also for insurance which resulted in more salary deductions. Choices should be given to employees rather than making such rules mandatory for everyone as everyone has different needs. 
Second is the variable pay which they call as Annual Cash Bonus. Now this amount is also deducted from the CTC which is given at the end of the financial year but not complete amount.

The firm does not treat their employees as people. 
Underpays by a big margin and does not promote employee growth. You may get a good offer when you start, but growing in the organisation is extremely difficult.
HR policies are borrowed from 1990's.  When you exitthe firm, they extract the last bit of juice from the employees. HR policies are intentionally kept hiden so employees can make use of it or plan accordingly. The appraisal cycle is designed in a way that by the time you exit, you have already spent  at best 6 months into the next year. 

Promotions and salary growth are kept at the bare minimum. I exited the firm after almost 5 years with no promotion and very little salary hike, this is after being highest performer in 2 out of 4 appraisal cycles I saw. 

The organisation is good if you want to enter at a good raise, but building career here is a bad choice from all angles.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Great place to learn what to do and what not to do as an engineer.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: RIL petrochemical plants are some of the best in the world in terms of technology and processes. It is, in my opinion, the best place to start working as a young engineering graduate (Mechanical, Chemical, Instrumentation and Electrical). There is a lot of opportunity to get first hand knowledge and experience in operating and maintaining world class machinery and technology. Probably the best place in India for a graduate to start his/her career. Company has developed a lot of processes and systems to standardise the work place. It has a good induction and training program. The management exerts a lot of control on the daily working of the plants. You cant get a better experience in petrochemicals than this in India. Has a very good brand image. Work is demanding and yet very rewarding in the Central Engineering services. Company has maintained townships for the employees. RIL is a launch pad for engineers trying to get placed in the middle east oil and gas sector companies after 5-6 years experience.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
The HR processes are surprisingly under developed in the case of lower and middle management. The efects of the HR back end operations are not felt by the employees. The visible HR activities are confined to reimbursing bills, travel arrangements etc. There is no evidence of an HR strategy in terms of employee career progressions, succession planning, and on how to assess the business impact of each employee. PMS still follows a bell curve and your annual rating pretty much depends on how well you market yourself, rather than the actual work you have done.

The work load and stress in the plant level operations and maintenance groups is tremendous. Employees have known to work for straight 36 hours without a break during shutdown activities. review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Not the best use of your time. Good enough for a short stint though.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Chance to experience work centered around the world's biggest grassroots refinery. Seriously, the place is huge, and the technology being used is indeed interesting and mostly world class. The chance to experience working as a cog in the machine, alongside gaining knowledge about the tools and methodologies used in the petroleum industry, is a valuable investment of time.

Some departments work on problems intellectually stimulating enough to be worth it. The are the people working on long term, unsolved problems, which have no published solutions. Others are engaged in routine tasks, the periodical oiling of the machine, the maintenance of checks and balances. Problem is, less than 1% of the highly skilled engineers are put into interesting work. These guys are also in charge of continuously improving the part of the refinery they're assigned.

If one can show significantly more economical solutions to problems, they will be weighed and processed and implemented in due course of time, as Reliance has ridiculous amounts of money to invest. One can easily envision a dream and get it implemented because of all the money.

Flat-ish hierarchy is a double edged sword. Good to know the chairman is not that many steps away. 

The benefits offered are generally typical of big companies.

The location is alright, has all the basic middle class amenities covered. Movie theater, all kinds of sports, parks, street vendors, exotic animals and crazier stuff. 

Vacation spots are interesting, and some are quite unique and offer once in a lifetime experiences.

Has an airport and a on-refinery helipad.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Almost all of the intellectual capital is outsourced to foreign companies, Indian engineers are mostly engaged in maintenance and improvement work. Interesting work is rare, as even though you're given free reign to explore and find better solutions, the lack of time and energy to pursue it may throttle such attempts. 

The bureaucratic slowdowns, at least in the place I worked, was extremely high. It is so ubiquitous that having a person who simply knows Gujarati will speed up tasks many-fold.

Remuneration is abysmal. It is so low a candidate of sufficient caliber will not choose to stay in this place for long. Thus, the flux of employees is high, lots of 20 year olds, very few 30 year olds (because they all left, as Reliance on their resume looks very good) and plenty of 40 somethings and older stuck with Reliance.

The location is a bit far from any 'urban' environment. The small-ish town of Jamnagar is 30 kilometers away, closest metropolis is Ahmedabad. Employees either have an 8*6 hour workweek or a 9.5*5 hour workweek. One typically spends 11 hours a day for work, including the commute. That leaves about 5 hours for leisure, which isn't really encouraging.

Vacation from here is limited, one could exhaust all possible weekend tourist spots in under a dozen weekends.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Maybe a world-class refining site, but it has its share of negatives.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
(i) At the Jamnagar site, you get exposure to the latest technological advancements in the oil & gas field. The refinery itself is the biggest grassroot refinery in the world.
(ii) The scope of learning never ends. The plant is pretty complicated as a whole, and the top management is bringing in modifications and upgrades to all its machinery to achieve greater throughput (and consequently, profit), which adds to the necessity of gathering the maximum amount information about the processes involved. Technical services provides exposure to a lot of modelling and simulation software viz. Aspen Suite, HTRI etc. In Jamnagar, the township is decent with all amenities available.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
If you're in operations, you're made to work REALLY HARD. Added to this, the work culture takes a back seat here. The babu culture is prevalent, and a lot of people try to cover up or dump their mistakes on others in order to climb up the ranks. Whichever level you work at, there will always be 5 people constantly pointing their fingers at you and asking for explanations.

Coming to the compensation, the average salary package is way below the competing PSUs. Annual salary increments through appraisals are also very less. Since it is a private company, perks are out of the scene. 

Moving on to the township, nonvegetarian food is strictly mediocre, plus Reliance Fresh does not sell any nonveg items. The nearest city is Jamnagar, where the only famous chains are Dominos & CCD. Nearest airport at Ahmedabad is 350 kilometres away, making the place way too isolated. Social life - not so much. And more importantly, Gujarat is a dry state.

1) Big brand name
2) Best technology in the industry
3) Migration opportunities to different verticals
4) Chances to settle to different location than one started.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1) Management: Generally people talk about top management being bad, middle management being bad but here everything is terribly wrong. The attrition rates are off the roof but somehow the company manages to hide these figures from media. And why not, they literally own the media.

2) Transparency: Since few years the company has stopped publishing the survey results which it conducts inside the company and one can guess why. There is a load of dissatisfaction among employees which the company is hiding for the fear of getting known. Also the rating systems are absolutely opaque and is a behind-the-closed-door activity.

3) The five day a week paradox: The company recently won praise from media (which they own) and the analysts (which they pay). But the reality is absolutely opposite and any manufacturing site can tell you why. The company cleverly moved its front entities in Mumbai to 5 day regime. They even moved the minor departments like accounts, mechanical, Instruments to the same. But the biggest chunk, the operations department and the projects were maintained at 6 day a week. Although the company claims to have reduced the working hours of 6 day personnel while in fact the very reason is to make people stay back after normal working hours (1 hour is extremely normal) on all six days which would not have been possible in a 5 day regime (and no extra pay of course)

4) Employee Value: The company boasts about values and behaviour but when it comes on trust on employee such things are flushed down the gutter. One of the examples is the banning of mobile phones with cameras and internet (banning the cameras could have sufficed, nevertheless) but then it allowed its Senior General Managers to flaunt any mobile of their liking. Does that means that SGM and above people are loyal and below are not. Moreover photography is strictly banned but in a recent high profile visit of its own employees, people were seen taking pictures over the backdrop of the refinery itself (excellent hospitality one would say).

5) Talent and Resignations: This is a closely knit cycle hence taken together. The company hires GETs from top colleges in india IITs, BITS, NITs and world renowned Nirma University. They add them together and put them under the mentorship of some excellent leftovers of technical wannabes. The problem is if you have someone giving you work who himself doesn’t understand it there won’t be results. Adding to this is rampant sycophancy. This leads to displeasure and departures of India’s finest and useless leftovers are ready to mentor the next lot and the cycle continues. The work allocation does not see potential but the amount of saliva on the boots. People from NITs and BITS have ended up climbing on ladders and noting down pressure gauge readings everyday while some ‘world renowned’ employees set out to solve technical issues.

6)HR and Admin: As the name suggests these are here to manage some of India’s finest and finest they are, from desi colleges (who after 3 years in Gujarat are still unheard of). Their language is impeccable and conduct courtly. These people found one of the pantry too noisy hence it was shutdown for ‘regular’ employees. The Admin people are found openly manhandling employees over petty issues and take pride in issuing threats of complain and meddling with ratings in the middle of Office area.

7) Knowledge: The people here have their own way of disseminating knowledge. There are no workshop or technical discussions esp. for personnel in Operations. No encouragement whatsoever is given to pursue technical objectives (in fact discouraged so that it doesn’t hamper with the ‘routine work’). The experience people get a free ride from the work of their juniors (the fresh graduates). Plagiarism is one thing, they eliminate the name of the author even remotely from the file and bypassing such hierarchy is a sin punishable by monitoring pressure gauges for the rest of remaining career.

8) Resignation: After a toiling effort when I managed to put in my papers I was surprised to find out that the company although relieves you within a month but makes you pay for the remaining period of 90 days even if the employee is ready to serve for the whole duration of 90 days. No questions asked and no clarifications offered.

9) Shutdowns: Shutdowns are  a nightmare here. Shutdown means 12 hours a day work continuing to the whole duration of the shutdown period of 50-60 days. Although normally it should be 4 day 12 hours followed by a leave but manpower shortage can be cited as a reason to continue this to 6 days (No extra pay of course). Also leaves are a rarity in this period unless you have an emergency wherein you have to give a detailed explanation of your brother’s or mother’s illness failing which one can end in partial or total cancellation of his leaves.

10) Internal Switch: There are myriad of job opportunities and lot of internal advertisement of the same. But the irony is that one cannot apply to an internal job unless approved by the superior. This makes it impossible for someone to apply for a internal job unless you have an understanding superior (which is a rarity). This deeply hampers the internal job procedure.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1.	Exposure to business and opportunities to work on traditional management methods which other orgs used during 1990's.
2.	HR who has no idea about their departments itself.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Finding a job outside with the skillset you gain at Reliance is almost impossible because, other companies use computers but here they use manpower in the name of generating employment. 
2. Work from Monday to Saturday starting morning 10 to night 8 . Even if you are a Female employee , your boss will still ask you to sit till late evening hours despite being a desk job . 
3. There are people who work Monday to Sunday 9 AM to 9 PM also as they don’t know what else to do because the only work you see is the cabin your whole life. Unfortunately, they will not get any other jobs outside and will become slaves of Reliance.
4. Mostly lower middle class are hired from Tier-2 , Tier-3 colleges who have lot of family responsibilities and are forced to work. Initial offer would be attractive, but over time when you realize that you are not getting any hike and skillset is not improving due to less exposure, you get stuck. 
5. I pity all those men and women who were forced to work in Reliance Retail due to family responsibilities and now struggling to get a life because work is all you do. I have seeing women crying at desk because of this culture and stuck in the org. They got kids who only see their mom 1 day a week.
6. A HR department who have no idea of what other employees do and we even doubt whether they cleared any exams or got a legit degree. 
7. A reimbursement system which makes sure that you do not get even 70 % what you claimed for. Employee ends up using their own salary for Reliance Company during company travels and engagements with clients. Worst part was HR themselves were not clear on the policies of reimbursement and other company processes. Shame on you.
8. Hybrid reporting makes sure that you do not learn anything or gain loyalty of even a single boss as everyone wants their work done without fail regardless of what the other boss is asking.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: RIL are known to undertake big-ticket projects and gives you good exposure when there is a project. Salary and reimbursements are credited on time because the company never suffers from any cash-crunch. It is an intersting company for MBA grads as it is into diverse businesses. Also, it pays returnees quite well as long as they are returning after a substantial hiatus, especially with loads of experience (international will boost your chances at negotiating better). Also, since there are several functions and departments, you do have the flexibility to look for better opportunites internally (purely from an experience perspective, not necessarily pay!). More importantly, you can count on the fact that your job is secure, as they hardly fire employees unless they are found to have done something unethical or illegal. It is indeed one of the biggest employers in India.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: RIL are way too poor paymasters and practise discriminatory policies, yet live in self-denial. The company is run by egotists and fiefdoms and employees often get sandwiched between them. You will really be lucky to get a good, effective manager. Those who seem to behave politely often have vested interests, so beware! As far as the pay goes, the discrimination is so rotten that you are likely to find a peer who enjoys twice as much salary as you do, not necessarily on the account of being a high performer, but due to either being a motormouth in meetings or a sycophant or anything else. To make it clearer, I still don't earn what some of my peers earned 4 years ago. If you openly differ with your manager or superior, you are asking for trouble. Just because I raised a certain point about something, I was forced to relocate to a less fancy location and the whole episode was packaged in such a way that it seemed that my services were better suited in the new location. Very few employees return due to bad blood. The management turns a blind eye to all these proceedings. Basically, RIL are a very unethical company to work for.
